title: 5C Prompt Contracts: A Minimalist, Creative-Friendly, Token-Efficient Design Framework for Individual and SME LLM Usage
author: Ugur Ari
extraction_notes: Proposes 5C Prompt Contract framework: Character, Cause, Constraint, Contingency, Calibration. Tested across OpenAI, Anthropic, DeepSeek, Gemini. Key finding: 5C achieves 54.75 avg input tokens vs 348.75 for DSL and 346.25 for unstructured. Demonstrates that minimal structure can maintain output quality while reducing token cost by 84%.
key_claims: 5C framework achieves 54.75 average input tokens vs 348.75 for DSL and 346.25 for unstructured across all LLMs tested
5C framework maintains comparable output quality (777.58 avg output tokens) while reducing input overhead by ~84%
Five components: Character (role), Cause (goal), Constraint (boundaries), Contingency (fallback), Calibration (output optimization)
Tested across OpenAI GPT, Anthropic Claude, DeepSeek, and Google Gemini
Minimalist structure preserves the LLM's 'entropy budget' for deeper semantic exploration
DSL-style prompts produce more concise but less creative outputs (711.50 avg output tokens)
Unstructured prompts produce highest output variance and least predictable adherence to user intent
